# Board Brief — Security Risk Posture

<!--
  Board Brief Template for Vigil SOC
  
  This template defines the structure for board-grade risk posture reports.
  The Reporter agent follows this structure when a user requests:
    "Generate board brief" or "Create board report"
  
  Customize this template to match your organization's reporting format.
  All sections are required unless marked (optional).
  
  DATA RULES:
  - Every metric must be pulled from actual findings and case data
  - Never hallucinate or estimate metrics — if data is unavailable, state "Data not available"
  - No CVE numbers or MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs in the main body
  - Use plain business language throughout
-->

**Report date:** {{date}}
**Period:** {{period_start}} — {{period_end}}
**Prepared by:** Vigil SOC — automated board brief

---

## Risk posture: {{POSTURE_INDICATOR}}

<!--
  POSTURE_INDICATOR is one of:
    🔴 RED    — Active breach or uncontained critical threats requiring immediate board attention
    🟡 YELLOW — Open critical findings with remediation in progress; elevated risk
    🟢 GREEN  — No open critical findings; remediation on track; risk within tolerance
  
  Determination logic:
    RED:    Any active/uncontained critical finding OR validated kill chain with no containment action
    YELLOW: Open critical findings exist but containment actions are submitted/in-progress
    GREEN:  Zero open critical findings AND mean remediation time within SLA
-->

**One-line summary:** {{posture_summary}}

---

## Key metrics

| Metric | Current period | Prior period | Change |
|--------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| Validated kill chains | {{kill_chains_current}} | {{kill_chains_prior}} | {{kill_chains_delta}} |
| Detection coverage | {{detection_coverage_pct}}% | {{detection_coverage_prior_pct}}% | {{coverage_delta}} |
| Mean time to remediation | {{mttr_current}} | {{mttr_prior}} | {{mttr_delta}} |
| Open critical findings | {{open_criticals}} | {{open_criticals_prior}} | {{criticals_delta}} |

<!--
  METRIC DEFINITIONS:
  - Validated kill chains: Count of cases containing 2+ findings that map to
    sequential MITRE ATT&CK tactics (e.g., Initial Access → Execution → C2).
    Falls back to critical-severity finding chains if ATT&CK data is unavailable.
  - Detection coverage: (Findings with associated cases / Total findings) × 100
  - Mean time to remediation: Average time from case creation to resolution
    for cases resolved in the reporting period. Expressed in hours or days.
  - Open critical findings: Count of findings with severity=critical and no
    resolved case.
  
  PRIOR PERIOD: Same-length window immediately before the current period.
  Example: if current period is last 30 days, prior period is 31-60 days ago.
  
  CHANGE: Use ↑/↓/— arrows and state whether the direction is favorable.
-->

---

## Top 3 action items

### 1. {{action_1_title}}

- **Risk:** {{action_1_risk_sentence}}
- **Requires:** {{action_1_fix_type}} <!-- budget | policy | technical -->
- **Impact if addressed:** {{action_1_impact}}

### 2. {{action_2_title}}

- **Risk:** {{action_2_risk_sentence}}
- **Requires:** {{action_2_fix_type}}
- **Impact if addressed:** {{action_2_impact}}

### 3. {{action_3_title}}

- **Risk:** {{action_3_risk_sentence}}
- **Requires:** {{action_3_fix_type}}
- **Impact if addressed:** {{action_3_impact}}

<!--
  ACTION ITEM RULES:
  - Derive from actual open findings and cases, prioritized by severity and business impact
  - Each item should be one the board can act on: approve budget, change policy, or direct
    a technical team to execute
  - "Risk" is one sentence a non-technical board member can understand
  - "Requires" is one of: budget, policy, or technical
  - "Impact" states the concrete improvement if this item is completed
  - If fewer than 3 actionable items exist, include only what the data supports
-->

---

## Trend: {{trend_direction}}

<!--
  TREND_DIRECTION: "Improving" | "Stable" | "Degrading"
  
  Based on validated exposure count (critical + high findings without resolved cases)
  across three windows: 0-30 days, 31-60 days, 61-90 days.
  
  Improving: Each 30-day window shows fewer open exposures than the one before
  Stable:    Less than 10% change across windows
  Degrading: Each 30-day window shows more open exposures than the one before
-->

| Window | Open exposures | Direction |
|--------|---------------|-----------|
| Last 30 days | {{exposures_30d}} | — |
| 31–60 days ago | {{exposures_60d}} | {{dir_60d}} |
| 61–90 days ago | {{exposures_90d}} | {{dir_90d}} |

**Trend summary:** {{trend_narrative}}
